div1.entryFree: Distemperature, disorder, 1) of the body: a huge infectious troop of pale --s, Err. V, 82. our grandam earth, having this d. H4A III, 1, 34. 2) of the mind: thou art uproused by some d. Rom. II, 3, 40. upon what ground is his d. Per. V, 1, 27 (Ff distemperance). 3) of the weather: thorough this d. we see the seasons alter, Mids. II, 1, 106.*how bloodily the sun begins to peer above yon bosky hill! the day looks pale at his d. H4A V, 1, 3.
